name: vscode-stm32-can
description: Build, flash, and diagnose STM32 firmware over CAN — one button, four adapters, live telemetry. Wraps the can-flasher CLI.

commands.title: Use CLI on PATH (ignore managed download)

iscFs.canFlasherPath.markdownDescription: Path to the `can-flasher` binary. Use the bare name `can-flasher` if it's on `$PATH`; otherwise an absolute path. When left at the default and `#iscFs.cliAutoDownload#` is on, the extension prefers a version-matched binary it downloads itself.

iscFs.cliAutoDownload.markdownDescription: Download a `can-flasher` binary matching this extension's version from the GitHub release and use it, instead of relying on whatever is on `$PATH`. Keeps flashing in lockstep with the extension (the desktop app stays in sync by compiling the same library in). Ignored when `#iscFs.canFlasherPath#` is set to a custom path — that is always honoured. Falls back to `$PATH` when offline or on an unsupported platform.

enumDescriptions: SLCAN serial — CANable and compatible adapters (all OSes)
PEAK PCAN — PCAN-Basic on Win/macOS, SocketCAN on Linux
Vector XL Driver Library — VN-series adapters (Windows)

iscFs.channel.markdownDescription: Adapter channel string passed as `--channel`. Examples: `/dev/ttyACM0`, `COM3`, `PCAN_USBBUS1`, `can0`, `0` (Vector index). Leave empty for `virtual`.

iscFs.nodeId.markdownDescription: Target node ID — hex (`0x0`–`0xF`) or decimal. **Flashing requires this** (the CLI won't guess which board to overwrite); if it's empty you'll be prompted at flash time and the answer is saved here. Team scheme: ECU = `0x1`, AMS = `0x2`, uDV = `0x3`. For broadcast commands like *discover*, an empty value omits `--node-id`.

iscFs.buildCommand.markdownDescription: Shell command run from the workspace root before flashing — equivalent to STM32CubeIDE's *build then flash* step. The default configures CMake's `build/` directory if needed (no-op when it already exists) and then runs the build, so a fresh clone Just Works. Replace with `make`, `west build`, `cargo build --release`, or any other one-liner that produces the artifact pointed at by `iscFs.firmwareArtifact`. Set to an empty string to skip the build step entirely.

iscFs.firmwareArtifact.markdownDescription: Path or glob to the firmware artifact (`.elf`, `.hex`, `.bin`) relative to the workspace root. Default `**/build/**/*.{elf,hex,bin}` finds the output of a typical CMake/Make project. Set explicitly (e.g. `build/firmware.elf`) for projects that produce many candidates; the extension prompts if a glob matches multiple files.

iscFs.requireWrp.markdownDescription: Pass `--require-wrp` to `flash` — fails if the bootloader sector isn't write-protected.

iscFs.applyWrp.markdownDescription: Pass `--apply-wrp` to `flash` — applies WRP if missing, then continues.

iscFs.profile.markdownDescription: Pass `--profile` to `flash` for per-phase timing output.

iscFs.jumpAfterFlash.markdownDescription: Pass `--jump` (default) to jump to the application after a successful flash. Disable to stay in bootloader mode.

iscFs.liveDataRateHz.markdownDescription: Rate at which the bootloader emits `NOTIFY_LIVE_DATA` snapshots to the live-data webview panel. Higher rates are smoother but increase bus load.

iscFs.liveDataWindowSeconds.markdownDescription: Sliding-window size (seconds) shown on the live-data chart's x-axis. Older snapshots scroll off the left edge.

viewsWelcome.contents:
No CAN adapters detected yet.

[Refresh](command:iscFs.refreshDevices)

[Select adapter…](command:iscFs.selectAdapter)

Make sure your CAN adapter is plugged in and the `can-flasher` binary is on PATH (or set `iscFs.canFlasherPath`).

For a no-hardware smoke test, set `iscFs.interface` to `virtual`.

walkthroughs.title: Get flashing with MingoCAN
walkthroughs.description: CLI ready → pick an adapter → find a board → your first flash over CAN.

steps.title: Confirm the CLI is ready
steps.description:
The extension manages a version-matched `can-flasher` for you. Run Doctor to confirm it — and the rest of your setup.
[Run Doctor](command:iscFs.doctor)

steps.title: Pick your CAN adapter
steps.description:
Choose which adapter to talk to your boards through.
[Select adapter…](command:iscFs.selectAdapter)

steps.title: Find a board on the bus
steps.description:
Broadcast on the bus and list the boards in bootloader mode.
[Discover devices](command:iscFs.discover)

steps.title: Your first flash
steps.description:
Build your firmware and flash it over CAN in one step.
[Build & Flash](command:iscFs.flash)
